title: Cell-type-specific epigenomics
text: echoannot includes data generated “Nott2019”: Nott , Holtman IR, Coufal NG, … Glass CK. Brain cell type-specific enhancer-promoter interactome maps disease-risk association. Science. 2019 Nov 29;366(6469):1134-1139. doi: 10.1126/science.aay0793. Epub 2019 Nov 14. PMID: 31727856; PMCID: PMC7028213.

title: Cell-type-specific epigenomics
text: Note: functions download remote bigwig files UCSC, requires network access may take several minutes.
code: superenhancers <- echoannot::get_NOTT2019_superenhancer_interactome() enhancers_promoters <- echoannot::NOTT2019_get_promoter_interactome_data() peaks <- echoannot::NOTT2019_get_epigenomic_peaks()

title: Cell-type-specific epigenomics
text: addition plot object, tables raw read ranges called peaks included output list.
code: dat <- echodata::BST1 histo_out <- echoannot::NOTT2019_epigenomic_histograms(dat = dat) knitr::kable(head(histo_out$data$raw)) knitr::kable(head(histo_out$data$peaks))

title: Cell-type-specific epigenomics
text: echoannot also includes data generated “Corces2020”: Corces, M.R., Shcherbina, ., Kundu, S. et al. Single-cell epigenomic analyses implicate candidate causal variants inherited risk loci Alzheimer’s Parkinson’s diseases. Nat Genet 52, 1158-1168 (2020). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-020-00721-x

title: Cell-type-specific epigenomics
code: bulkATACseq_peaks <- echoannot::get_CORCES2020_bulkATACseq_peaks() cicero_coaccessibility <- echoannot::get_CORCES2020_cicero_coaccessibility() hichip_fithichip_loop_calls <- echoannot::get_CORCES2020_hichip_fithichip_loop_calls() scATACseq_celltype_peaks <- echoannot::get_CORCES2020_scATACseq_celltype_peaks() scATACseq_peaks <- echoannot::get_CORCES2020_scATACseq_peaks()

title: Cell-type-specific epigenomics
code: peak_dat <- echoannot::granges_overlap(     dat1 = dat,     chrom_col.1 = "CHR",     start_col.1 = "POS",     dat2 = scATACseq_celltype_peaks,     chrom_col.2 = "hg38_Chromosome",     start_col.2 = "hg38_Start",     end_col.2 = "hg38_Stop") ggbio::autoplot(peak_dat,                 ggplot2::aes(y=ExcitatoryNeurons, color=Effect))

title: Getting Started
text: Annotate SNP-wise fine-mapping results. , ’re annotating small number SNPs high-confidence causal SNPs demo purposes. SNPs supply annotate_snps, longer take query selected databases SNP. Note: chunk kept eval=FALSE annotate_snps queries external databases (HaploReg, RegulomeDB, BioMart), can slow may rate limits.
code: #### Only query high-confidence fine-mapping SNPs from one locus #### dat <- merged_DT[Locus=="LRRK2" & Consensus_SNP==TRUE,] #### Query annotations #### dat_annot <- echoannot::annotate_snps(dat = dat,                                       haploreg_annotation = TRUE,                                       regulomeDB_annotation = TRUE,                                       biomart_annotation = TRUE) knitr::kable(dat_annot)

text: Data publication contains results cell type-specific (neurons, oligodendrocytes, astrocytes, microglia, & peripheral myeloid cells) epigenomic assays (H3K27ac, ATAC, H3K4me3) human brain tissue. detailed metadata, see: Built-datasets: Enhancer/promoter coordinates (GenomicRanges) PLAC-seq enhancer-promoter interactome coordinates API access full bigWig files UCSC Genome Browser, includes Epigenomic reads (GenomicRanges) Aggregate epigenomic score cell type - assay combination

title: Run GoShifter enrichment pipeline — GOSHIFTER
text: locus_dir Path locus-level results directory. dat data.table data.frame columns SNP, CHR, POS, P. SNP_group Character string labelling group SNPs (default ""). goshifter_path Path directory containing goshifter.py. NULL, bundled copy shipped echolocatoR used (see GOSHIFTER_find_folder). permutations Number permutations enrichment test (default 1000). ROADMAP_search keyword query passed ROADMAP_query filter ROADMAP annotations (e.g. "monocyte"). chromatin_states Character vector chromatin state abbreviations test enrichment (default c("TssA")). R2_filter LD r-squared threshold GoShifter (default 0.8). overlap_threshold Minimum number overlapping SNPs required running GoShifter annotation (default 1). force_new_goshifter TRUE, re-run even results already exist disk (default FALSE). remove_tmps Remove intermediate GoShifter output files collecting results (default TRUE). verbose Print messages (default TRUE). save_results Write combined results tab-delimited file (default TRUE).

title: Brain cell type-specific enhancers, promoters, and interactomes — get_NOTT2019_interactome
text: doi:10.1126/science.aay0793 file <- file.path(     "~/Desktop/Fine_Mapping/echolocatoR/annotations",     "NOTT2019/aay0793-Nott-Table-S5.xlsx" ) sheets <- readxl::excel_sheets(file) enh_prom_sheets <- grep("enhancers|promoters", sheets, value = TRUE) other_sheets <- grep("enhancers|promoters", sheets,     value = TRUE,     invert = TRUE ) NOTT2019_interactome <- lapply(other_sheets, function(s) {     readxl::read_excel(file, sheet = s, skip = 2) }) NOTT2019_interactome <- append(     NOTT2019_interactome,     lapply(enh_prom_sheets, function(s) {         readxl::read_excel(file,             sheet = s, skip = 2,             col_names = c("chr", "start", "end")         )     }) ) names(NOTT2019_interactome) <- c(other_sheets, enh_prom_sheets) #### piggyback #### tmp <- file.path(tempdir(), "NOTT2019_interactome.rds") saveRDS(NOTT2019_interactome, tmp) piggyback::pb_upload(     file = tmp,     repo = "RajLabMSSM/echoannot" ) NOTT2019_interactome <- get_NOTT2019_interactome()
